evidence: Table 1 specifies RF variable importance as "Gini index." The paper states: "To determine which biomolecules were most predictive of the case/control status of each individual, the features were ranked within each source using a variable importance metric appropriate for each classification algorithm." Table 5 presents ranked importances and the Discussion concludes: "In terms of the variables selected by the different methods (Table 5), CLR and CSVM-RBF selected the same 2 metabolites as the 2 most important features: alpha-tocopherol and apidic acid...Both methods found these 2 metabolites to be highly important." The paper employs no permutation, SHAP, PDP/ALE, conditional, or heldout validation of the Gini-based ranking. Feature sets include metabolites, lipids, and SNPs with scales ranging -0.079 to 23.149 (metabolites) vs 0 to 2.0 (SNPs).
